new_classes.label_en: Still fermented beverage from concentrated grape must in containers ≤ 2 L (subheading 2206 00 51)
new_classes.label_de: Anderes gegorenes Getränk, still, aus konzentriertem Traubenmost, in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von ≤ 2 L (Unterposition 2206 00 51)
new_classes.definition_en: Still fermented beverage produced from concentrated grape must (not from natural fermentation of fresh grape must) presented in containers holding 2 litres or less. Fermentation is typically started with yeast; sugar may also be added. Falls under CN heading 2206.

new_classes.label_en: Fermentation process from concentrated grape must (production process for subheading 2206 00 51/59)
new_classes.label_de: Gärungsprozess aus konzentriertem Traubenmost (Herstellungsverfahren für Unterposition 2206 00 51/59)
new_classes.definition_en: Fermentation process in which concentrated (stable) grape must is used as the starting material, with fermentation typically initiated by yeast and possibly with added sugar, resulting in a beverage classified under CN heading 2206.

new_classes.label_en: Concentrated-grape-must-origin quality (beverage produced from concentrated grape must, not natural fresh-grape fermentation)
new_classes.label_de: Eigenschaft 'aus konzentriertem Traubenmost' (Getränk nicht aus natürlicher Gärung von Frischtraubenmost)
new_classes.definition_en: Quality borne by a fermented beverage indicating that it was produced from concentrated grape must rather than from the natural fermentation of the must of fresh grapes.

new_classes.label_de: Eigenschaft 'nicht schäumend' (stilles gegorenes Getränk, weder Schaum- noch Perlwein)
new_classes.definition_en: Quality borne by a fermented beverage indicating that it is still (neither sparkling nor semi-sparkling), used to discriminate between sparkling (2206 00 31) and still (2206 00 51/59/81/89) subheadings within heading 2206.

coverage_explanation: The main distinguishing criteria are covered: (1) the beverage is still (non-sparkling), captured by StillFermentedBeverageQuality; (2) it is produced from concentrated grape must rather than natural fresh-grape fermentation, captured by ConcentratedGrapeMustOriginQuality and the linked process class; (3) it is presented in containers ≤ 2 L, captured via the existing SmallContainer class through a has-part restriction. The note also mentions that fermentation is typically started with yeast and sugar may be added – these are process details that cannot be fully expressed as OWL class restrictions without introducing highly specific named process parameter classes; they are described in the process class definition but not axiomatised as formal restrictions. The German note refers to HS Explanatory Notes items 1–10 for heading 2206, which are not individually modelled here. Coverage is therefore approximately 0.8: the three main structural discriminators (still, concentrated-must origin, container ≤ 2 L) are formally represented, but the production parameter details (yeast, sugar addition) and the broader HS explanatory note cross-reference are not axiomatised.
